message:
test: search-surface regression tests covering structure, interaction, and visual hierarchy (2dccg.8.6)

14 new tests protect search bar, filter pills, breadcrumbs, and footer HUD
against regressions. Covers mode-aware titles, pill style tokens, breadcrumb
emphasis, footer KBD pairing, F-key mode cycling, escape unwind chain, and
degradation matrix. Includes snapshot baselines and prior uncommitted doc/test
additions from find bar contract (2dccg.4.1) and baseline snapshots (2dccg.6.2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.1","title":"Wire STYLE_KBD_KEY and STYLE_KBD_DESC into footer key-hint rendering","description":"## Goal
Use dedicated keyboard legend tokens instead of generic status/muted styles so footer hints are semantically themed and consistent.

## Evidence
`src/ui/app.rs` currently styles key hints with `STYLE_STATUS_INFO` and `STYLE_TEXT_MUTED` near footer render logic, while `STYLE_KBD_KEY`/`STYLE_KBD_DESC` exist in `style_system.rs` but are unused.

## Work Items
- Update hint span builder call sites to use `STYLE_KBD_KEY` and `STYLE_KBD_DESC`
- Validate readability in all 6 theme presets
- Verify degradation fallback remains legible without high color fidelity

## Acceptance
- Footer key hints no longer depend on generic status token
- Token audit reports keyboard tokens as actively used
- No regressions to contextual hint formatting or wrapping","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:02.412846253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:27:52.290705560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:49:28.553427290Z","close_reason":"Implemented: KBD tokens wired into footer","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:27:50.580323366Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:02.412846253Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":348,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.2","title":"Complete role-gutter rendering for user/tool/system alongside assistant","description":"## Goal
Restore per-role gutter differentiation so all message roles receive explicit visual markers, matching or exceeding old ratatui clarity.

## Evidence
Current rendering only applies `STYLE_ROLE_GUTTER_ASSISTANT` in list/detail area logic, while user/tool/system gutter tokens are defined but not rendered.

## Work Items
- Identify gutter render pipeline and role-selection logic
- Apply role-specific gutter styles for User, Agent, Tool, System
- Validate alignment and spacing with long/wrapped content
- Ensure accessibility mode markers remain coherent with color cues

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3","title":"Track C: Make markdown rendering theme-coherent with active UI preset","description":"## Objective
Remove theme mismatch inside the detail pane by aligning markdown rendering with the currently selected UI theme preset.

## Why This Exists
`MarkdownRenderer::new(MarkdownTheme::default())` is instantiated in multiple paths, causing markdown code blocks/headings/emphasis to ignore selected theme (Dark/Light/Catppuccin/Dracula/Nord/HighContrast).

## Scope
- Introduce theme mapping strategy from style context/preset -> markdown theme
- Use mapped theme in all message/detail markdown render paths
- Ensure runtime theme switching immediately affects markdown output

## Deliverables
- No hardcoded default markdown theme in detail rendering
- Theme-consistent markdown in both cached-detail and fallback-content paths
- Regression tests for at least dark/light/high-contrast and one accent-heavy preset

## Completion Criteria
- Review screenshots show markdown colors blending naturally with surrounding pane
- Theme toggle in TUI updates markdown look without stale artifacts
- No clippy/lint regressions introduced by the refactor","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:29.957388638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.043359342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:29.957388638Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.013539998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":251,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track C Intent
This track eliminates theme mismatch introduced by hardcoded `MarkdownTheme::default()` in detail rendering.

### Design Constraint
Markdown styling must be coherent with active UI theme *and* remain readable under high-contrast/accessibility modes.

### Reliability Requirement
Theme switching should immediately affect markdown output; stale renderer/theme state is considered a regression.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.1","title":"Design and implement UiThemePreset -> MarkdownTheme mapping strategy","description":"## Goal
Create a robust mapping that aligns markdown renderer palette with the selected UI preset instead of relying on `MarkdownTheme::default()`.

- Decide mapping source: explicit preset map vs derived colors from `StyleContext`
- Ensure support for all presets: Dark, Light, Catppuccin, Dracula, Nord, HighContrast
- Define fallback behavior for custom overrides/theme-editor changes

## Deliverables
- Mapping utility with clear API surface
- Documentation comment explaining color/contrast trade-offs

- High-contrast preset preserves accessibility targets

## Files touched
- src/ui/style_system.rs: Added STYLE_PILL_INACTIVE (pill.inactive) and STYLE_PILL_LABEL (pill.label) tokens with definitions in build_stylesheet
- src/ui/app.rs: Renamed active_filter_pills() → filter_pills() to include inactive placeholders; refactored build_pills_row() from monolithic String to per-pill Spans with label/value split; updated rendering call site

## Behavior changes
- Filter pills now show all available filter slots (agent, ws, time, source) — active filters show as bold/accent, inactive show as muted/subtle

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","title":"Add search-surface regression tests (structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy)","description":"## Goal
Protect search-surface rendering with tests that verify structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy remain correct after polish changes.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead is the **single owner** of search-surface regression coverage.
- Includes: search bar, filter pills, breadcrumbs, footer HUD, and search-input affordance behavior.
- Excludes: results-surface regression ownership (`2dccg.9.5`), global interaction state-machine routing ownership (`2dccg.11.2`), cross-theme/degradation matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`), and full workflow E2E playback (`2dccg.11.3`).
- Must reuse baseline fixtures/helpers from `2dccg.6.2` instead of duplicating baseline snapshot setup.

## Test Scenarios

### Structure Tests
- Search bar renders with mode-aware title and cursor position.
- Filter pills render with correct style tokens (STYLE_PILL_ACTIVE for active, standard for inactive).
- Breadcrumb trail renders with clickable segments and correct emphasis.
- Footer HUD renders with KBD key/desc token styling.
- Layout respects LayoutBreakpoint constraints (search bar width, pill wrapping).

### Interaction Tests
- Tab key cycles through filter pills in expected order.
- Enter key on search bar triggers search.
- Click on pill toggles active/inactive state.
- Escape key clears search input or exits mode.
- Mouse hover on interactive elements shows correct cursor affordance.

### Visual Hierarchy Tests
- Active filter pills have higher visual weight than inactive.
- Search bar input area is visually distinct from surrounding chrome.
- Footer key hints maintain KBD/desc pairing across themes.
- Theme switch updates all search-surface styles immediately.

## Logging
- Each test case logs: test_id, component, action, expected_state, actual_state.
- Failures include terminal size, active theme, degradation level.
- Use structured logging from `2dccg.11.6` when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All search-surface regressions from the ftui migration are caught.
- Tests run in < 3s total and require no terminal (headless).
- Test names clearly indicate which search-surface element is being validated.
- Suite composes with `2dccg.6.2`/`2dccg.6.3`/`2dccg.11.x` without duplicate ownership.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:32.127079571Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:16.298074887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:36.523677893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:40.892002824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:46.311618406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:52:05.096187123Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

## What changed
- `src/ui/app.rs`
  - `ResultItem` snippet pipeline now has an explicit fallback chain:
    - `hit.snippet` -> `hit.content` -> `hit.title` -> `"<no snippet>"`.
  - Added width-safe wrapping helpers:
    - `snippet_source()`
    - `char_boundary_at()`
    - `wrap_break_index()`
  - Reworked `snippet_lines()`:
    - hard width cap now respects narrow terminals (`max_width.max(8)` instead of forcing 20)
    - avoids empty wrapping artifacts
    - uses deterministic truncation (`...`) when line budget is exhausted
  - Added density-aware snippet budgeting:
    - `snippet_line_budget(max_width)`
    - compact/cozy/spacious behavior can now diverge intentionally under narrow widths.
  - Added compact-mode inline snippet context:
    - `compact_snippet_preview(max_width)`
    - row-height 2 now uses `score + source + snippet preview` (instead of metadata-only), restoring useful context in compact density.

## Tests added
- `result_item_snippet_fallback_chain_prefers_snippet_then_content_then_title`
- `result_item_snippet_wrapping_respects_narrow_width_without_empty_artifacts`
- `compact_density_results_row_includes_inline_snippet_preview`
- `spacious_density_allocates_three_snippet_lines_when_width_allows`

- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

## Acceptance mapping
- Snippets provide useful context in compact and spacious modes: ✅
  - compact rows now surface inline preview text.
  - spacious rows reserve multi-line snippet budget.
- No broken wrapping artifacts on narrow terminals: ✅
  - width-aware wrapping + truncation verified with narrow-width test.
- Snapshot-like deterministic structure validation: ✅

## Tradeoffs / residual risk

- Full visual snapshot diff coverage for snippet structure is still owned by `coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5`; current lane provides deterministic behavioral tests as a stable base.

+## Detail Find Bar Token Contract (2dccg.4.1)
+
+### Semantic tokens
+- `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_CONTAINER` (`detail.find.container`): container surface for the inline find row in detail pane.
+- `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_QUERY` (`detail.find.query`): active query text emphasis.
+- `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_ACTIVE` (`detail.find.match.active`): current match indicator (high-emphasis state).
+- `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_INACTIVE` (`detail.find.match.inactive`): total/secondary match count (low-emphasis state).
+
+### Part → token mapping
+- Find bar row container/background: `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_CONTAINER`
+- Query text after `/`: `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_QUERY`
+- Current match segment (`current/total` current portion): `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_ACTIVE`
+- Remaining match metadata (total, no-match state): `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_INACTIVE`
+
+### Theme and degradation expectations
+- Theme-aware: all four tokens derive from semantic theme palette (`surface`, `overlay`, `accent`, `selection_*`, `text_muted`) and therefore follow preset changes automatically.
+- `DegradationLevel::Full` / `SimpleBorders`: render full token styling (container background + emphasized query/current match).
+- `DegradationLevel::NoStyling` / `EssentialOnly`: rendering layer falls back to structural/plain text while preserving information order and keyboard behavior (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`).
+
+### Contrast and focus guidance
+- Current match state must remain visually stronger than inactive match metadata.
+- Container background must be distinct from surrounding detail content in all presets.
+- Query emphasis must not rely on color alone (bold retained for emphasis in mono/high-contrast modes).
+
 ## Accessibility & Resilience
 - Colorblind support: role gutters pair color with pattern (solid vs dotted vs dashed) in gutters when `CASS_A11Y=1`.
 - Mouse optional; all interactions keyboard-first.
